title: [Feature] Add event/callback system for order lifecycle and trade notifications
body:
## Problem

The engine has no way to notify external systems about what happened. Callers only get trades back from `SubmitOrder()`. There is no notification for:
- Order accepted (added to book)
- Order partially filled
- Order fully filled
- Order cancelled
- Order rejected
- Order book state changes

This makes it impossible to build real-time UIs, audit logs, or downstream processing pipelines without polling.

## Proposed Solution

Add an event callback system:

```go
type EventType int

const (
    OrderAccepted EventType = iota
    OrderPartiallyFilled
    OrderFilled
    OrderCancelled
    OrderRejected
    TradeExecuted
)

type Event struct {
    Type      EventType
    Symbol    string
    Order     *Order
    Trade     *Trade
    Timestamp time.Time
}

type Engine struct {
    // ...
    eventHandler func(Event)
}
```

Alternatively, use a channel:
```go
events chan Event
```

This enables:
- Real-time WebSocket feeds
- Trade persistence to database
- Audit logging
- Metrics and monitoring

## Priority
Medium — foundational for any production use case.
